key_points: Light is a form of energy that helps us see objects
A shadow is formed when an object blocks light
Opaque objects do not allow light to pass through them
Transparent objects allow light to pass through them completely
Reflection occurs when light bounces off a surface
examples: Shadow formed when sunlight falls on a tree
A mirror reflects light and allows us to see our image
formulas: Reflection → Bouncing back of light from a surface
Shadow → Dark region formed when light is blocked by an object
exam_tips: Understand the types of materials: transparent, translucent and opaque
Practice simple diagrams showing shadow formation
Learn basic ideas of reflection using mirrors
